item.title: Pricing the oil inventory drawdown
item.description: Could Brent crude hit $140 per barrel?

item.title: Investors wake up to the merits of sleep biotech
item.description: More people worry about poor sleep than excess weight, but drugmakers have been deterred from entering the market by costs

item.title: On yer bike! Freud and middle-aged men in Lycra
item.description: The ever-present fear of ridicule among cycling’s weekend warriors tells us a thing or two about human nature

item.title: In World Cup season, choose your financial trophies carefully
item.description: Real Madrid’s president has made it very clear that investing in the club is mainly about having fun and making contacts

item.title: Wasting China’s solar panel surplus is madness
item.description: Clean power is within our reach — yet factories sit idle

item.title: Can AI save a company’s soul?
item.description: It is easy to imagine institutional memory lost to automation preserved in silicon — but there is one crucial difference

item.title: Are US Treasuries becoming a financial chokepoint?
item.description: A haven asset for the financial system is becoming a source of risk

item.title: Why bother with university?
item.description: As costs soar and graduate prospects dim, the value of higher education can no longer be taken for granted

item.title: Why images of beauty matter in dark times
item.description: Even amid the challenges of the here and now, we find ways to survive — and celebrate that survival

item.title: Ireland’s plucky wine underdogs
item.description: Winemaking on the Emerald Isle is no easy feat, yet the country’s small but growing number of vignerons are up to the task

item.title: Politicians and the bond markets: lost in translation
item.description: Mutual misunderstanding and panicky commentary mask a joint desire to see the UK economy doing better

item.title: Sturgeon-gate should prompt couples to question their partner’s finances
item.description: Couples who do Excel together can exceed together

item.title: ‘Let’s just call it damn good’: Jay Rayner reviews Wild Izakaya, London
item.description: High-concept gloss belies a surprisingly laid-back City venue with serious cooking

item.title: The ethical dilemmas of AI
item.description: How we address the revolution is a question of how we manage its uncertainties

item.title: The avoidable tragedy of Henry Nowak
item.description: This case resonates because it plays into two sets of public fears

item.title: The dollar’s decline is overstated — but still real
item.description: The most reliable measure of dollar dominance is probably the loans and other dollar obligations created outside US

item.title: How can I avoid the World Cup?
item.description: It’s the longest ever. What’s a non-football fan to do?

item.title: The Musk exception on banking fees for SpaceX
item.description: Most companies are still likely to pay full price on smaller US deals as a prestige good

item.title: My returns have been exponential — no, seriously!
item.description: The speed at which my portfolio is rising in value is ridiculous

item.title: The CPG blues
item.description: Bad luck and bad decisions in consumer products

item.title: How much value is AI really creating?
item.description: Eye-opening changes to the speed and volume of work are not always translating into genuine productivity

item.title: Governments need to learn how to talk about debt
item.description: They must find a way to tell taxpayers how their money is spent

item.title: The big backlash over plans to drop US quarterly reporting demands
item.description: Enraged investors have sent flood of letters to SEC to express their opposition

item.title: How Britain could afford to pay for war
item.description: Keynes’s 1940 financing plan contains lessons for the current government

item.title: Men need a little vanity
item.description: Caring about how we look, within limits, helps everyone
